desc: gpt-5.4 聚类,41事件,session-key对齐(可算B-cubed)

events.title: Set up Claude Code OAuth in Safari
events.summary: The user completed Claude Code setup in Safari by visiting the Anthropic authorization callback URL and finishing account connection. The flow included connecting their Anthropic profile, handling subscription/session state, and reaching the confirmation screen that said Claude Code was ready to use.

events.title: Researched audio capture architecture across apps
events.summary: The user investigated audio capture and recording architecture spanning My-Portrait and My-Meeting. They reviewed files and concepts such as Audio/Recording/RecordingManager.swift, captureService.startStreams, AudioDevice, deviceService.availableDevices(), CoreAudio tap, system-vs-mic recording, and screen-recording permissions. The work was exploratory and comparative rather than a single code fix.

events.title: Verified videoFps setting was unused
events.summary: The user traced the capture.screen.videoFps setting through Sources/MyPortrait/Settings/ConfigSchema.swift, ImageLoader.swift, TimelineView.swift, and compaction-related code. They concluded that videoFps did not actually affect screenshot capture or MP4 compression behavior, so the slider was effectively a dead setting. They then considered removing the related UI/config surface from ScreenConfig and adjacent code.

events.title: Discussed modeling personal evolution over time
events.summary: The user used Claude to think through how a personal memory system should represent long-term human development. They contrasted 'snapshot' versus 'movie' models, talked about extracting identity-formation events from continuous data, and referenced ideas like '20岁的他', '25岁的他', '30岁的他' and 'Stanford式反思'. The discussion was philosophical but tied directly to product design for My-Portrait.

events.title: Integrated PostHog analytics in MyMeeting
events.summary: The user worked in Xcode on AnalyticsService.swift for the MyMeeting project. They configured the PostHog SDK with the API key and server URL https://us.i.posthog.com, while also checking related constants such as AppConstants.bundleID and logging setup. This was a focused analytics integration task.

events.title: Asked Claude assorted technical side questions
events.summary: The user used Claude as a technical sounding board for several side questions during development. Topics included Xcode handling multiple projects/windows and same-device testing, Rewind AI failure analysis, AMFI boot-args, API capabilities, LCEL, and other ad hoc engineering questions. These sessions were consultative research rather than changes to one specific feature.

events.title: Ported screenshot OCR workflow to Swift
events.summary: The user reviewed and ported screenshot OCR logic into Swift for My-Portrait, repeatedly using claude, sourcekit-lsp, swift build, and portrait.sqlite inspection. They examined OCR-related records and confidence values like IDs 2497-2501, dealt with a Python string-formatting syntax error in /tmp, and referenced UI/code surfaces such as MemorySettingsView, MemoriesView, InputCaptureView, and config.toml. Some sessions also touched My-Meeting, but the consistent task was translating and validating the OCR pipeline in Swift.

events.title: Reworked scheduler and memory timing settings
events.summary: The user reviewed scheduling and timing controls in My-Portrait, including scheduler jobs, backoff/tick intervals, quiet hours, and daily-budget behavior. They inspected MemorySettings-related functions such as frequencyRow, timeRow, weekdayRow, and dayOfMonthRow, weighing whether to simplify the model or support true time-based execution. This was a configuration-design pass rather than a bug investigation.

events.title: Added Balanced power mode and auto indicator
events.summary: The user implemented and verified a power-profile feature in My-Portrait with modes including Auto, Performance, Battery saver, and Balanced. They worked on PowerProfileState, @Observable/SwiftUI updates, recomputePowerProfile, and committed the change affecting files like SettingsModel.swift and PowerProfile.swift, including a 500ms screenshot interval and JPEG quality 0.60 for Balanced. They also checked live UI behavior in Auto mode so the current effective gear would be shown responsively.

events.title: Built and tested MyMeeting recording templates
events.summary: The user developed and validated recording-template workflows in My-Meeting. They worked through template creation, editing, deletion, applyTemplate behavior, system-vs-mic recording settings, per-meeting model/summary configuration, imported audio, and repeated build verification including a successful '** BUILD SUCCEEDED **'. Concrete entities included meetings like 'Meeting 2026-06-18-20-10', 'Meeting 2026-06-02-09-25', DROP 1/DROP 2, and template names such as '模板名'.

events.title: Fixed writing-capture message boundary loss
events.summary: The user debugged a writing-capture bug in WritingCaptureWorker.swift where sent text could be lost around placeholders and event boundaries. They investigated extractSentMessages, endValue, bestGroupText, baseline-skip heuristics, and concrete cases around IDs 1150, 1153, and 1154, including text like 'Write message..' and '我是想做全环境覆盖的_'. The work culminated in commit 6143423, which they staged and committed after real-world validation of multi-event send handling.

events.title: Researched IME-safe multilingual typing capture
events.summary: The user investigated how writing capture should handle IME behavior, especially pinyin composition and tail-loss when commit and send happen too close together. They discussed cases like 'jiu s s', 'dian-店', CJK segmentation limits, OCR-plus-keystroke alignment, and the desire to support Japanese, German, and Arabic without forcing input-method switching. This combined product-design reasoning with low-level timing ideas such as submitRaceBurst and adjusted read points.

events.title: Added event folder menus and color management
events.summary: The user worked on event-folder and memory-management UI in My-Portrait. They explored EventFolder model/store CRUD, color and name persistence, right-click menus for folders/events, deletion confirmation, and related changes in MemoriesView.swift, with commit references including 95ebe8c. The task also included thinking about reset/delete flows and search/context-menu state in the memories interface.

events.title: Messaged about selling household items on WeChat
events.summary: The user had a WeChat conversation with Lucifer about selling a metal shelf for $15 and a baby bed for $6. This was a brief personal logistics chat that stood apart from the surrounding development work.

events.title: Played music while working
events.summary: The user played 'Empty old City' by ゴースト警告を唄う on Spotify during the work session. The playback happened alongside ongoing OCR and writing-capture research notes, but the distinct action here was listening to music.

events.title: Shared a Claude referral and quick calculation
events.summary: The user used WeChat to message contacts including 王昱 and share a Claude referral link: https://claude.ai/referral/CGzNBuyoKQ. They also did a quick arithmetic check, computing 23.13 + 97.43 = 120.56, while surrounded by development context.

events.title: Captured meeting screenshots with Snipaste
events.summary: The user used Snipaste to capture and annotate screenshots tied to meeting artifacts and transcript segments. Visible anchors included meetings like 'Meeting 2026-06-18-20-10', 'Meeting 2026-06-02-25', 'The daily show segment', DROP 1/DROP 2, and assets such as 1095.HEIC. The activity centered on visual capture and annotation rather than coding.

events.title: Configured provider connections and import settings
events.summary: The user worked on configuration and build verification for AI-provider connections and import-related UI across My-Portrait and My-Meeting. They touched provider setups for Codex, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Groq, OpenRouter, and ElevenLabs, checked avatar/icon presentation, and reviewed import-scanning controls such as 'Auto Scan Everything From Imports' in ImportSettingsView.swift. The sessions also included swift/Xcode build checks while being careful about parallel-session conflicts.

events.title: Debugged stuck pending audio chunks in transcription queue
events.summary: The user investigated why transcription could stall when pending audio files were missing. They traced the issue through AssetPath.resolve, PortraitDBImpl.swift, and pendingAudioChunks filtering with compactMap, noting that missing files like chunk 3402 could remain forever pending and block the queue. Related sessions included work in TranscriptionScheduler.swift, wav synchronization, SQLite cleanup of recordings, and testing on-device transcription in the app.

events.title: Added Screenpipe branding assets to My-Portrait
events.summary: The user looked at the screenpipe GitHub repository in Safari and then integrated a Screenpipe icon into My-Portrait’s Assets.xcassets. They created Screenpipe.imageset, added a 1024px screenpipe.png plus Contents.json metadata, and regenerated/build-tested the Xcode project. The task was a small branding/asset integration effort rather than core logic work.

events.title: Refined portrait writing-style and memory distillation logic
events.summary: The user examined portrait-related writing_style markdown files such as chat_brevity.md, bilingual_register_split.md, and delete_burst_revision.md while thinking about memory-pipeline reliability. They referenced source_record_ids, portrait weighting/EMA concepts, staged writing styles, and approval flows like pendingReview/Approve. The work centered on how distilled personality/style signals should be refreshed and validated from OCR-derived records.

events.title: Fixed voice training card blocked/error reset behavior
events.summary: The user debugged a voice-training issue where the UI could remain in a blocked or stale error state and prevent retraining. They investigated SpeakersSettingsView.swift, VoiceTrainingCard behavior, microphone permission checks like micGranted, and an older "got 0s" audio-format problem from versions before 1.0.113. The fix involved resetting trainer/error state on appear so the card reflects the current session correctly.

events.title: Linked Codex and researched Claude Code options
events.summary: The user handled account/tooling setup in Safari by linking ChatGPT to Codex through auth.openai.com and then researching Claude Code. They searched for "claude codex," browsed the Anthropic Claude Code product page, and reviewed related resources and repositories such as cc-switch and everything-claude-code. This was a short exploratory/tool-enablement thread rather than app coding.

events.title: Configured Claude desktop profile details
events.summary: The user edited their Claude desktop profile, setting their full name to Zhuoyi Zhang, nickname Joy, and avatar initials to ZZ. They also specified background/preferences such as being a Chinese user in the U.S., using a macOS M5 machine with 24 GB RAM, arm64, and zsh. This was a one-off identity/profile setup step.

events.title: Explored keystroke-plus-OCR canvas architecture in Claude
events.summary: The user used Claude as a design partner to reason about a canvas pipeline where keystroke transcription is primary and OCR acts as a correction layer. They discussed hard constraints on content localization, CJK handling, AMFI/SIP-related feasibility questions, and notes like 'canvas_fusion' and 'keystroke_log' while comparing approaches to document fidelity.

events.title: Browsed AI tools and study materials in Safari
events.summary: The user casually navigated among Claude, ChatGPT, Screenpipe, DeepSeek, and study-guide pages in Safari. They looked at items like '4-Page Study Guide - v2', 'Physics Study Guide - Electricity', '测试逻辑功能', app icon requests, and homework pages rather than working on a single coding fix.

events.title: Checked Claude account usage and routine limits
events.summary: The user opened Claude account usage details in Safari to inspect session and weekly quotas. They saw indicators such as 100% current session usage, 46% weekly usage for all models, 4% Sonnet usage, and daily routine runs at 0/15.

events.title: Deleted a Claude chat thread
events.summary: The user briefly managed their Claude chat history and confirmed deletion of a conversation. The dialog showed 'Are you sure you want to delete this chat?' while nearby history included technical chats like 'Xcode同时打开多个项目' and 'Rewind AI失败原因分析'.

events.title: Browsed gaming and social content on WeChat
events.summary: The user spent a short WeChat session browsing posts and files rather than messaging about work. They viewed promotions for games like '绝区零 3.0' and '第五人格', saw content from 王昱 and 黑马程序员, and opened files including valis-logo.png, video.mp4, and chapel_view_tour.mp4.

events.title: Reviewed Discord conversations and call notes
events.summary: The user checked Discord messages and voice-call related notes involving people such as Blair, Joy, Stan, ff_kai, 何成, and Edward. They referenced a June 4 call, a summary file named '会议总结-Valis-2026-06-05.md', and ongoing personal and collaboration chat threads.

events.title: Submitted a late school final exam assignment
events.summary: The user briefly handled a school task in Chrome by opening the Canvas assignment FINAL EXAM MINI LAB and using New Attempt before submitting. The page showed the course path chccs.instructure.com/courses/58112/assignments/250791, a due date of May 29 at 11:59pm, and the downloaded file FINAL EXAM MINI LAB.docx. This was a distinct one-off submission rather than development work.

events.title: Debugged MyMeeting transcription and speaker training
events.summary: The user iterated on MyMeeting's audio pipeline, comparing FluidAudio with silero_vad.onnx, tuning context/window values like 64 and 512, and testing files such as 'Personal ai meeting 1.m4a' and '声音训练素材'. They added LocalizedError handling for cases like 'noSpeechDetected' and 'sampleRateUnsupported', retrained speaker fingerprints from stored audio, and verified builds and commits from Terminal and Calendar-linked workflows.

events.title: Reverted a My-Portrait cron/history change
events.summary: The user used Terminal to revert commit 40b13e9 in My-Portrait after investigating a problem around cron-trigger recognition and the frontend history button's sensitivity. They checked TimelineSidebar.swift while working from a My-Portrait shell session assisted by Claude.

events.title: Fixed light-mode divider visibility in My-Portrait
events.summary: The user adjusted SwiftUI styling so separators were no longer hard-coded as Color.white.opacity(x), replacing them with Color.primary.opacity(x) across views like MarkdownView, ContextPickerView, CronJobsView, and TimelineView. They committed the change as 'fix(theme-light): 分隔线不再硬编码白色, light 模式下可见' and verified the UI in My Portrait.

events.title: Researched multilingual writing capture and lost text recovery
events.summary: The user investigated writing_records problems in My-Portrait, especially a missing line '你得抓住mei' and how to recover only user-authored text from edit logs. They discussed IME-safe capture for Japanese, German, and Arabic, used tools like extractSentMe, and worked with portrait.sqlite and baseline-removal rules such as '只记你打出来的' and '删除基线已有内容'.

events.title: Optimized My-Portrait transcript view rendering
events.summary: The user fixed lag in My Portrait's transcriptions view by replacing ScrollView plus LazyVStack with a virtualized List using .listStyle(.plain). They validated the performance improvement on long speaker re-identification cases and tied it to previously observed main-thread freezes.

events.title: Sampled a MyMeeting hang during speaker ID
events.summary: The user investigated a MyMeeting freeze by running sample MyMeeting and saving stacks to ~/Desktop/mm-hang.txt. They inspected hangs involving Thread 0, SIGTERM behavior in Xcode, and possible interactions with OnnxRuntime, FluidAudio, and speaker-identification code paths.

events.title: Optimized MyMeeting long-transcript rendering
events.summary: The user improved MyMeeting responsiveness on long meetings by switching transcript rendering from ScrollView plus LazyVStack to List with plain styling. They tested on items like 'DROP 1', 'DROP 2', and 'Meeting 2026-06-06_18-20-10', while re-identifying speakers and checking related database and FTS behavior.

events.title: Documented features to port from MyPortrait
events.summary: The user wrote an Obsidian note titled 'MyPortrait 可移植到 MyMeeting 的功能清单' to organize features worth porting into MyMeeting. The note focused on speaker identification improvements, including FluidAudio-based separation, renaming speakers to real names, and retroactive regrouping of transcript segments.

events.title: Browsed the My-Portrait GitHub repository
events.summary: The user briefly reviewed the public GitHub repository for My-Portrait in Safari, looking at files like README.md, DEVELOPER.md, Package.swift, project.yml, docs, and test folders. They also viewed repository metadata such as commits, branches, releases, issues, and pull requests.

events.title: Chatted casually in a Discord server
events.summary: The user spent a short session in Discord chatting in the 头尖尖 server's #皇片 channel with people such as DOT Bave, 炫彩哈基蜂, and 孙美川258. The exchange included playful messages, reactions to media, and casual social chatter rather than work coordination.
